varieties.name: Grosse Lisse
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Excellent subtropical. Sets fruit hot conditions. Improved disease resistance. Australian standard 50+ years.
varieties.availability: Widely available: Qld nurseries, Bunnings, Diggers, Eden

varieties.name: Tommy Toe
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Outstanding subtropical, humidity adapted, disease tolerant. Diggers winner since 1993. Must-have for Brisbane.
varieties.availability: Qld nurseries, Diggers, Eden, Green Harvest

varieties.name: Green Zebra
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Good winter cropper, tolerates cooler temps. Distinctive appearance, good disease resistance humidity.

varieties.name: Roma / Mini Roma
varieties.flesh_fruit: Meaty, low moisture, mild, few seeds
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Excellent subtropical, heat/humidity tolerant. Ideal sauces, canning. Mini Roma compact. Good disease resistance.
varieties.availability: Very common - all Qld nurseries, Bunnings

varieties.name: Black Russian
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Grows well but fruit fly prone - exclusion netting essential. Distinctive dark, excellent flavor. Best netted humid summers.

varieties.name: Mortgage Lifter
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Personal best performer. Strong staking required. Good subtropical. Excellent slicing.

varieties.name: Black Cherry
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Bred for warm humid Florida - perfect! Excellent disease/heat tolerance. Vigorous, productive. Sow Mar-Sep.

varieties.name: Apollo Improved
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Excellent mild winters. Improved bacterial wilt, nematode resistance. Firmer fruit. Good all-season subtropical.

varieties.name: Beefsteak
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Performs well with support. Heavy fruits need staking. Good heat. Excellent fresh, sandwiches.

varieties.name: Scorpio
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: BRED FOR QUEENSLAND! Tolerates humid subtropical/tropical. Resistant bacterial/fusarium wilts. Local adaptation excellent.

varieties.name: Scoresby Dwarf (KY1)
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Australian heritage, locally adapted. Compact for small gardens/containers. Good disease resistance humidity. Excellent sauces.

varieties.name: Cherokee Purple
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Loves wet heat - perfect humid summers! Not dry heat. Excellent disease resistance. Solid production. Rich complex. Green shoulders normal.

varieties.name: San Marzano
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive long hot, excellent disease
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Top heat-tolerant performer. Incredibly productive long hot seasons. Excellent disease resistance humidity. Perfect canning, roasting, sauces.

varieties.name: Chadwick Cherry / Camp Joy
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Excellent hottest summer (Jan-Feb). Rare ability set fruit extreme temps. Good backup heat waves.

varieties.name: Bite Size
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Disease resistance package. Train to three leaders. Vigorous long season.

varieties.name: Sun Gold
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Popular subtropical. Very sweet, children love. Prolific warm season. Good humidity.

varieties.name: Sugarlump Cherry
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Extensively tested excellent autumn. One of best for Brisbane. Good subtropical. Attractive ombre.

varieties.name: Thai Pink Egg
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Asian tropical origin excellent subtropical. Heat/humidity adapted. Unique appearance. Good warm humid performance.

varieties.name: Amish Paste
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: One of best for sauces. Performs subtropical with disease management. Heat tolerant. Excellent cooking, canning.

varieties.name: Pink Brandywine
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Better heat tolerance. Needs consistent watering, partial shade hottest months. Continues setting fruit heat. Best heat-tolerant heirloom flavor.

varieties.name: Pruden's Purple
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Early maturity ideal - harvest before intense heat. Lower cracking humidity. Solid disease. Good autumn for spring harvest.

varieties.name: Black Krim
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Performs well subtropical. Attractive dark. Rich complex. Good heat. Needs netting birds/fruit fly humidity.

varieties.name: Costoluto Genovese
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Italian warm climates similar Brisbane. Distinctive ribbed attractive. Excellent fresh/cooking. Good subtropical.

varieties.name: Gardener's Delight
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Excellent subtropical. Long harvesting extended season. Sweet family favorite. Reliable producer.

varieties.name: Pusa Ruby
varieties.traits: Semi-determinate, round, thick glossy skin, yellow stem end
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Highly popular, most widely grown India. Adaptable to climatic changes and soil types. Good pest resistance, thrives extreme conditions. Suitable spring-summer, autumn-winter. Both table and processing.
varieties.availability: Widely available all Kerala nurseries, IARI

varieties.name: Arka Rakshak
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: HIGH-YIELDING & DISEASE-RESISTANT. Crossing high-yielding F1. Resistant to ToLCV, bacterial wilt, early blight. Specifically developed for South India. Excellent tropical monsoon performance.
varieties.availability: Kerala nurseries, IIHR, Indian Agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Arka Abhijith
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good taste, suitable fresh and processing
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding for fresh market. Developed by IIHR specifically for Indian conditions. Good disease resistance. Performs well tropical monsoon.

varieties.name: Arka Samrat
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich taste, texture ideal for ketchup/puree
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Popular for making ketchup and puree. Determinate - all fruit ripens together for processing. Good tropical adaptation. Uniform quality.

varieties.name: Arka Saurabh
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding hybrid. Medium-sized juicy fruits. Preferred for adaptability to various climatic conditions including Kerala monsoon.

varieties.name: Arka Shrestha
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, long shelf life (17 days), easy transport
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding hybrid. Particularly good for Kerala due to long shelf life in humid conditions. Easy transport. Kharif/Rabi season. Ripens after Rabi.

varieties.name: Arka Vikas
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Early-maturing for Kerala conditions. Firm texture important for transport in monsoon. Suitable fresh market. Good tropical performance.

varieties.name: Arka Ananya
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding with excellent disease resistance - critical for Kerala monsoon. Medium-sized round red. Favourite among Kerala farmers.

varieties.name: Arka Vishal
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: ONE OF HIGHEST YIELDING. Fruits firm, round, dark red. Resist fruit cracking - important Kerala heavy rains. Excellent monsoon performance.

varieties.name: TNAU Tomato Hybrid CO 3
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: DEVELOPED FOR SOUTH INDIA. Adapted to hot humid conditions similar to Kerala. Good disease resistance for monsoon. Regional breeding program.
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu suppliers, South Indian seed companies

varieties.name: Pusa Gaurav
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, juicy, suitable fresh and processing
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Hybrid with good disease resistance. Favoured by Kerala farmers for high yield potential. Adaptable to tropical monsoon conditions.

varieties.name: Pusa Early Dwarf
varieties.traits: Determinate, compact, round yellow stem end, furrowed
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Early growing cultivar. Quick returns for Kerala farmers. Suitable both spring-summer and autumn-winter. Good for table and processing.

varieties.name: Pusa Sadabahar
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Known for continuous fruiting throughout year. Excellent for Kerala's year-round growing potential. Reliable production in tropical monsoon.

varieties.name: Pusa Uphar
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Determinate with early maturity. Round red fruits. Chosen for early maturity and canning suitability. Good Kerala adaptation.

varieties.name: Pusa Hybrid 4
varieties.traits: Uniform fruit size, excellent shelf life
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Popular hybrid. Uniform size and excellent shelf life important for Kerala humid conditions. Commonly used fresh market.

varieties.name: Pusa Hybrid 8
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, juicy, suitable fresh and processing
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Known for adaptability to various climates including Kerala tropical monsoon. Firm and juicy. Both fresh consumption and processing.

varieties.name: Abhinav
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Semi-determinate with excellent foliage cover. Square smooth dark red fruits. Fantastic foliage cover protects from intense Kerala sun. Good monsoon adaptation.

varieties.name: Namdhari
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Determinate early mature hybrid. Attractive glossy red colour. Sowing August-October. Cultivated across almost all Kerala districts. Very popular.

varieties.name: Rashmi
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Determinate widely adapted hybrid. Bright colouring. Suitable for processing. Good Kerala adaptation. Reliable first harvest 70 days.

varieties.name: Vaishali
varieties.traits: Medium-sized (100g) quality fruits, suitable making fresh juice
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: SUITABLE FOR HOT AND HUMID WEATHER - perfect Kerala! Determinate hybrid. Medium-sized quality fruits. Excellent for table and juice making.

varieties.name: Rupali
varieties.traits: Medium (100g) round, firm, smooth, good quality
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Determinate compact-growing early hybrid. Good foliage cover. Medium-sized high quality. Appropriate for processing. Good Kerala monsoon performance.

varieties.name: Roma
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Renowned for Indian farmers. Versatile, adaptable various Indian soils. Plum-shaped ideal sauces. Thrives well-draining soils, can withstand heat. Perfect Indian tropical climate.

varieties.name: Cherry Tomato (Various) 🌱/
varieties.traits: Small, sweet, various colours, good containers
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Gained immense popularity India. Sweet taste, capability grow various Indian soil conditions. Small size ideal small spaces, containers, transport, preserve. Suitable Kerala summers.

varieties.name: Bangalore / Bettina
varieties.flesh_fruit: Powerful resistance diseases, pests common South India
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: SPECIFICALLY DEVELOPED FOR SOUTH INDIA! Perfect fit Indian tropical climate. Thrives range soil types. Endurance resist bacterial wilt, other microorganisms. Reliable choice Kerala farmers seeking consistent yields.
varieties.availability: Bangalore nurseries, South Indian seed companies

varieties.name: Pearl Tomato / Cherry Pear
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Awesome choice Kerala farmers. Oblong shapes, small sizes visually pleasing. Preferred sweet taste. Grow containers, hanging baskets. Suitable urban Kerala gardening.

varieties.name: Punjab Kesari
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding known for pest/disease resistance. Medium-large tomatoes suitable processing. Good adaptation to Kerala if available.
varieties.availability: Punjab suppliers, may be available Kerala

varieties.name: Punjab Chhuhara
varieties.traits: Pear shape, red, firm, thick skin, seedless
varieties.yield_notes: 325 qtl/acre, 7 days shelf life after harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Seedless pear shape. Marketable quality 7 days - good Kerala humid conditions. Suitable long-distance transportation and processing.

varieties.name: Punjab Upma
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: SUITABLE CULTIVATION IN RAINY SEASON - perfect Kerala monsoon! Oval medium-sized firm deep red. Good yields.

varieties.name: Syngenta Saaho
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: SPECIFICALLY BRED FOR HOT HUMID TROPICAL OPEN-FIELD - perfect Kerala! Delayed ripening, enhanced transportability, hardiness deliberately selected. 'Breeding by design' for Kerala conditions. Disease/pest tolerant.
varieties.availability: Syngenta dealers, major Kerala seed companies

varieties.name: Syngenta TO-1057
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding semi-indeterminate. Exceptional fruit quality. Adaptability to various climatic conditions including Kerala tropical. Resistance to major diseases. Thrives tropical and temperate.

varieties.name: Syngenta TO-6242
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Higher yield potential, premium fruit quality. Longer shelf life - important Kerala humidity. Semi-determinate adapts diverse climates. Extended fruit setting. Enhanced disease resistance. Up to 20% higher yields.

varieties.name: Seminis Abhilash
varieties.traits: Good disease resistance, adaptable hotter climates
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: F1 SPECIALLY DEVELOPED FOR RAINY SEASONS - perfect Kerala monsoon! Exceptional disease resistance. High yields. Adaptable hotter climates. Early maturity 65-70 days from transplanting. Reliable rainy season choice.

varieties.name: Paiyur-1
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Type developed crossing Pusa Ruby and CO3. SUITABLE FOR RAINFED CULTIVATION - important Kerala monsoon areas. High-yielding determinate. Uniform ripening.
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu suppliers, South Indian seed companies

varieties.name: Pusa Hybrid 1
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Hot-set hybrid. Sets fruits above 20°C. Good for Kerala tropical temperatures. Reliable in heat.

varieties.name: Avalanche
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Cold-set. Sets fruits below 15°C. Suitable Kerala high-range areas with cooler mountain climates.

varieties.name: Pusa Swarnim
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: High-yielding hybrid. Good disease resistance important Kerala monsoon conditions. Reliable performance.

varieties.name: CO-3
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Developed Coimbatore for South Indian conditions. Good disease resistance. Regional adaptation ensures Kerala suitability.

varieties.name: Ernakulam Market Variety
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Popular in Ernakulam markets. Good for commercial cultivation. Suitable transport. Adapted central Kerala commercial conditions.

varieties.name: Kochi Urban Variety
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Adapted to urban Kochi conditions. Good for container growing in urban Kerala settings. City-adapted strain.

varieties.name: Palghat Gap Variety
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Specifically adapted to Palakkad gap climate. The gap between Western Ghats affects climate. Unique adaptation to gap conditions.

varieties.name: Kerala Monsoon Special
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: Modern hybrid specifically developed for Kerala monsoon conditions. Enhanced disease resistance for heavy rainfall. Good yields during challenging monsoon period.
varieties.availability: Modern Kerala seed companies, hybrid specialists

varieties.name: Athirappilly Variety
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: From Athirappilly region with very high rainfall. Adapted to extremely wet conditions. Good for Kerala's wettest areas.
